feedback.text: I think an important part that should be considered part of the Digital Fairness Act is the protection of consumers in regards to the digital, as well as physical copies of video games they own, ensuring that companies will keep their releases in a playable state. To think that it is currently possible for large multi-million video game companies to revoke your purchased license by shutting down access to the game itself, for any reason at all, is harrowing, especially when taking into consideration that they have all the funds in the world to keep the game running for fans who still want to play what they own.
